'''Jeff Wittenberg''' (born 19 March 1973) is an [[Australians|Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in [[England]] and [[Australia]]. His father [[John Wittenberg|John]] was an Australian international.

==Playing career==
Wittenberg began his career playing for the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]], a club who his father had also represented.<ref>[http://www.history.wynnumseagulls.com.au/ Club History] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111204083838/http://www.history.wynnumseagulls.com.au/ |date=4 December 2011 }} ''wynnumseagulls.com.au''</ref> In 1993, he signed with the [[St. George Dragons]], another of his father's former clubs. Wittenberg only made one appearance for the club and left at the end of the season.<ref name=dragons>[http://www.leagueunlimited.com/dragons/history/history_players_wxyz.php Players W-X-Y-Z] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111012080430/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/dragons/history/history_players_wxyz.php |date=12 October 2011 }} ''leagueunlimited.com''</ref>

In 1995, Wittenberg joined the new [[South Queensland Crushers]] franchise and played in their inaugural match on 11 March 1995. He spent two seasons at the club. Chosen for his size , at 196 cm he was the biggest man in the [[Crushers]] engine room. In 1997, despite interest from [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], Wittenberg signed with the [[Bradford Bulls]] for 1997's [[Super League II]].<ref>[http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Rugby+League%3A+Harris+hits+back.-a061142410 Harris hits back] ''Sunday Mirror'', 23 March 1997</ref> He only remained one season with the Bulls, joining the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 1998.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-super-powers-of-league-guide-to-the-top-divisions-12-teams-from-the-bulls-to-the-warriors-1153996.html Guide to the top division's 12 teams from the Bulls to the Warriors] ''The Independent'', 2 April 1998</ref> At the end of the 1998 season Wittenberg was released.<ref>[https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-teambyteam-guide-to-super-league-1078496.html Team-by-Team Guide to Super League] ''The Independent'', 5 March 1999</ref>

Wittenberg spent several years in the lower grades, playing for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and then the [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref name=dragons/><ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1096472.stm Dragons lack fire] ''bbc.co.uk'', 1 January 2001</ref> Wittenberg then returned to Huddersfield, playing for the club in the 2002 and 2003 seasons.<ref>[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2430307/Hayes-scores-two-tries-as-Huddersfield-cruise.html Hayes scores two tries as Huddersfield cruise] ''The Telegraph'', 17 February 2002</ref><ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/squads/ 2003 Super League squads] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090109165835/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/squads/ |date=9 January 2009 }} ''sportinglife.com''</ref>
